title: Grid Operator Bias Coverage Lab (EnergyTech)
purpose: Profile of the Evidence Bundle spec scoped to pre-deployment + ongoing-monitoring bias / equity-coverage evidence for AI tools used by electric utilities, grid operators, and pipeline operators in load shed allocation, outage restoration prioritization, demand response participation, EV charging deployment, rate-class design, energy-efficiency program targeting, and low-income program enrollment. Anchored to EPA Title VI environmental justice + Justice40 Initiative + state PUC environmental-justice impact assessment requirements (CA, MA, IL, WA) + CalEnviroScreen / NY DAC / EPA EJSCREEN designation methodologies. UNLIKE prior verticals' bias labs which focus on individual decisioning, this lab focuses on POPULATION-LEVEL equity in essential service delivery — the harm is uneven distribution of critical infrastructure outcomes, not uneven treatment of individual applicants.

ej_designation_epa_ejscreen.source: EPA EJSCREEN environmental justice screening + Justice40 Initiative (Climate and Economic Justice Screening Tool, CEJST)

state_disadvantaged_community_designatio.source: State-specific designations — CalEnviroScreen 4.0 (CA), NY Climate Act Disadvantaged Communities (DAC), MA EJ Population Designation, IL Energy Communities, WA Health Disparities Index

energy_burden_band.source: LEAD Tool (DOE Low-Income Energy Affordability Data) — % of household income spent on energy

lmi_designation.source: AMI (Area Median Income) per HUD designation

critical_medical_equipment_household.source: Utility-reported medical baseline + critical-care customer registry

limited_english_proficiency.source: Census American Community Survey + utility customer language preference

additional_dimensions.rationale: Spatial granularity for load-shed + restoration + DR + EV charging equity
Distinguishes IOU vs publicly-owned vs cooperative territory patterns
Distribution-level outcome attribution — feeder-level equity patterns
Restoration-priority equity — measure not just IF restored but HOW LONG
EV equity — count of public chargers per 1000 residents

coverage_status_taxonomy.description: n >= threshold AND gap <= threshold AND disparate-impact ratio >= 0.80
Disparate-impact ratio < 0.80 — state PUC environmental-justice impact assessment trigger; agency civil rights office referral pathway evaluation REQUIRED
EnergyTech-unique: load shed events systematically favor non-DAC tracts — explicit alert; mandatory state PUC + DOJ Civil Rights notification consideration
EnergyTech-unique: outage restoration time systematically longer in DAC tracts — explicit alert
EnergyTech-unique: registered critical-medical-equipment households had outage response time materially worse than baseline
EPA Title VI environmental-justice finding pending (utility federal-financial-assistance recipient overlay)
State PUC environmental-justice impact assessment failed (CA D.24-06-008 / MA DPU 24-15 / WA UE-240122)

non_obligations_explicitly: This bundle does NOT cover individual customer credit-decisioning bias — that's FinTech.
This bundle does NOT cover insurance underwriting bias for energy-related risks (storm damage, etc.) — InsurTech.
This bundle does NOT cover EEOC employment-bias for utility employees — HR Tech.
